Description: In response to 'banditry' (i.e., the Circle of the Inner Grove), Archduke Lyra Silvershield authorizes Upper City Watch patrols along the Western Gate's new logging routes outside the city. This is a controversial overreach, encroaching on Flaming Fist jurisdiction and angering Lord Durinbold, who sees it as using city resources for private gain.

The Parliament holds a public hearing on the stalled Refugee Relief Act. It devolves into a shouting match between nativist Patriars, merchants who want cheap labor, and a small contingent of moralists. High Watcher Alena of New Elturel gives a fiery, but ultimately ignored, testimony.

Lord Corin Durinbold's attempt to publicly sponsor the dispossessed Hlath family fails. Amnian Kontor agents, moving with speed and ruthlessness, intercept the family first. The Hlaths, intimidated or bought off, publicly refuse the Patriar Heritage Society's aid, making Durinbold look weak and outmaneuvered in a quiet but significant political defeat.

To enforce the new Harbor Dredging Levy, the Chionthar Consortium uses its influence to have the Flaming Fist impound a trading vessel belonging to 'Mad' Meggan of the Free Traders of the Outskirts for non-payment, making an example of her.

During the continued shakedowns at Wyrm's Crossing, a merchant from the Free Traders of the Outskirts who refuses to pay the 'toll' is brutally beaten by a Flaming Fist patrol led by Manip Jerrol. The incident is witnessed by dozens and sparks outrage among the Outer City communities.

The Watch's attempt to crack down on 'unsanctioned art' in the Upper City proves ineffective. The artists, more clever than anticipated, easily evade the clumsy patrols. The effort results in no arrests and is quietly abandoned, while mocking caricatures of Watch officers begin to appear on walls near the Citadel.

With their new warehousing monopoly, Master Joric the Stout's Consortium triples the storage rates for goods originating from the Sembian Kontor, claiming Sembian cargo requires 'additional security'. This is a direct economic attack.

The Sembian mercenaries hired by Matron Lyra Silvershield have their first skirmish with the Circle of the Inner Grove near the Whisperwood Copse. A druid is wounded, and two mercenaries are injured before the Circle melts back into the woods.

The Iron Throne's brokered expedition into the Undercity ends in disaster. The adventuring party they hired is ambushed and wiped out by a Guild patrol, who were seemingly tipped off. The heirloom is lost, the merchant is ruined, and the Iron Throne loses both its investment and a degree of credibility in the underworld.

Quest: Sell the Hlath Estate to an Ally
Description: Consul Vorlamin Athar's plan to quickly sell the Hlath Estate to an ally is thwarted. Lord Corin Durinbold of the Peerage of Blood discovers an ancient, obscure property law and files an injunction, tying the sale up in the city's labyrinthine bureaucracy. The estate remains empty, a symbol of the Amnian Kontor's rare but frustrating legal defeat.

Factor Elara Mornwood's scholars begin translating a key text 'acquired' from the Elturian refugees: the 'Tome of the Companion,' which details the binding of the celestial that guarded Elturel. They hope to find clues to powerful, forgotten binding magics.

Noticing the rising tensions, Bursar Korgan Ironhand tasks his intelligence network with recruiting a new asset inside the Flaming Fist barracks at Wyrm's Rock, hoping to get advance warning of any potential 'disruptions to trade' (i.e., protests or riots).

To recover the mechanical parts stolen by the Wharf Rats without admitting a security breach, Chief Artificer Glim-Nimblewhiz uses a third-party fence to post an anonymous but generous bounty for 'lost clockwork mechanisms' at the Elfsong Tavern.

Citing the new Harbor Dredging Levy, Master Helma Tallowshand's guild announces that their drydocks will no longer service vessels without a Shipwrights' Company seal, effectively forcing independent captains to either join the guild or risk their ships falling into disrepair.

Master Borin Stonehand's attempt to blame the shoddy wall repair on 'Zhentarim sabotage' is met with widespread public skepticism. Ettvard Needle of the Baldur's Mouth prints the claim but frames it as dubious, and without evidence, the story fails to gain traction. Instead of securing more funds, Stonehand's reputation for honesty takes a hit among the populace.

Quest: Bribe a Peerage of Coin Member
Description: To head off a proposed tax on luxury goods, Master Belba Quickfoot delivers a 'gift' of several casks of priceless Amnian wine (acquired from the Undercellar) to a key member of the Peerage of Coin, securing his vote.

Mistress Elethra Vance's internal security identifies a journeyman tailor who has been selling designs for the patriars' winter gowns to a rival boutique in Bloomridge. The journeyman is quietly fired and blacklisted from the trade.

Quest: Create a Masterwork for a Patriar
Description: Master Theronius Goldhand personally oversees the creation of a magnificent gold-and-silver canary automaton for Lord Durinbold, a gift meant to solidify the alliance between the traditionalist guild and the Peerage of Blood.

The Alchemists' Company's refusal to provide antitoxin to the Open Hand Temple is rendered moot. A resourceful group of adventurers bypasses the guild entirely, acquiring and delivering a large shipment of medical supplies from a Chionthar Consortium warehouse directly to the overwhelmed clinic in Rivington, earning the public's praise and the guild's private fury.

Following the dockworker strike, Matron Marga Saltwind negotiates a backroom deal with the Chionthar Consortium. The Fishmongers' fleets will be exempt from the Harbor Dredging Levy in exchange for guaranteed lower prices on fish for the Consortium's own mess halls.

Dammon hears a rumor of a tiefling in New Elturel who possesses a shard of a devil's contract, physically manifested during the Fall of Elturel. He seeks to purchase it, believing the infernal script can be used in his forge-work.

The attempt to silence a minor patriar and 'lose' a land deed inconvenient to the Western Gate Trading Company fails. The patriar avoids the planned 'accident' and successfully files the ancient deed with the city magistrates. This creates a massive legal and financial crisis for the Trading Company, as their claim to a key piece of property is now publicly and legally disputed.

The Guild Wardens' attempt to intimidate a city health inspector backfires. The inspector, either exceptionally brave or having secured protection from a rival faction, is not cowed by the threats. He files a damning report on the tanneries' pollution, resulting in a hefty fine for Goodman Borlu and unwanted official scrutiny of his operations.

In exchange for protection for her lamplighter crews in dangerous neighborhoods, Mistress Anya Brightwood provides a Guild ward boss with a list of streets where lamps are 'malfunctioning,' creating dark zones perfect for ambushes or smuggling.

Quest: Scry on a Peerage of Blood Member
Description: To gain leverage in the parliament, the Master of the Sodality has one of his wizards perform a scrying ritual on Lord Corin Durinbold, hoping to uncover personal secrets or political plans that can be used against the traditionalist bloc.

In response to the Zhentarim's arson, Nine-Fingers sends her hulking enforcer, Tusgront, to the Brampton district. He doesn't start fights; he just visits every shop, reminds them of the benefits of Guild protection, and suggests it would be 'unwise' to associate with newcomers.

Quest: Smuggle a Person Past the Gates
Description: The smuggling operation to get a disgraced patriar's son out of the city fails. A Flaming Fist patrol, acting on an anonymous tip, ambushes the smuggling party in the sewer tunnels. Silas the Eel's crew manages to escape, but the patriar's son is captured and imprisoned, causing a major scandal for his family.

One-Eyed Jack's plan to divert a Flaming Fist weapons shipment goes awry. Security on the transport is unexpectedly high, and the Guild's dockside contact fails to create the necessary opening. The shipment of crossbows arrives safely at the Fist barracks, leaving the Guild empty-handed and their informant network potentially compromised.

Roah Moonglow's agents quickly set up a new gambling den and loan-sharking operation in Brampton, the 'Velvet Hand,' right across from the ashes of the Guild tavern they burned. It's a blatant show of force, daring The Guild to respond.

The Harper investigation into the Masons' 'sabotage' claim stalls. Master Stonehand, paranoid about scrutiny, keeps the collapse site under heavy guard by his own Guild Wardens. The Harper agent is unable to gain access to gather conclusive evidence, leaving the Nightingale without the proof needed to counter Stonehand's public narrative.

The attempt to recruit the dispossessed Lord Hlath into the Knights of the Shield fails. At the clandestine meeting, Hlath balks at the infernal nature of the required oaths, proving too cowardly or too principled to trade his soul for revenge. He flees the meeting, leaving the Knights without their potential high-profile pawn.

Nansi Gretta's attempt to acquire the Chionthar Consortium's upcoming shipping manifests fails. The Consortium, wary of industrial espionage, has recently upgraded its internal security. The Bibliophile's agent is unable to access the documents, leaving both the Sembian Kontor and the River-Rats without the valuable intelligence they were expecting.

Quest: Investigate Psychic Disturbances near the K'liir
Description: The Gravekeepers' investigation into psychic disturbances near the K'liir yields no results. The Hand of the Absolute's cell is well-hidden, and their rituals leave no physical trace. After several nights of fruitless searching, Hemlock's agents dismiss the event as a passing psychic echo from the Githyanki camp, and the cult's activities go unnoticed.

The Bhaal cult's planned murder of a Flaming Fist informant is botched. The informant proves more resilient than anticipated, fighting off his attackers and escaping with non-lethal injuries. The cultists are forced to flee before completing the kill, failing their test and leaving behind a terrified, but living, witness who reports the attack to the Fist.

The Sharran agent's attempt to ritually cleanse the entrance to the old sanctum fails. The ancient wards protecting the sanctum prove too powerful for the lone agent to overcome. The ritual fizzles harmlessly, and the entrance remains magically sealed, a major setback for the Silent Shroud's plans.

The Hand of the Absolute's attempt to kidnap a fortune-teller from the Wide is a failure. The target, a woman of surprising resilience, creates a scene that draws the attention of a nearby Watch patrol. The cultists are forced to abandon their plan and flee, leaving them without the psychic focus needed for their ritual.

The Gilded Vein's attempt to manipulate grain futures collapses. An alert clerk at the Counting House spots inconsistencies in the forged manifests and raises an alarm. The Sembian Kontor, alerted to the scheme, quickly releases official statements that stabilize the market. The plot fails, costing the Gilded Vein significant capital and exposing their methods to their rivals.

Quest: Plant 'Evidence' of a Refugee Murder Plot
Description: The plot to frame the Hellriders for murder falls apart. While the cultists succeed in killing the merchant, a random Watch patrol happens upon the scene before they can properly plant the forged letter. The cultists are forced to flee, leaving the body without the incriminating 'evidence.' The death is reported as a simple, albeit tragic, robbery.

Mister Smiles's attempt to host a dream-inducing performance fizzles. The complex fey magic he weaves into the illusions is unstable, resulting in a confusing and lackluster show rather than a profound emotional experience for the audience. The cult fails to harvest any significant emotional residue, leaving their fey patron unsatisfied.

The Takers' assassination of master goldsmith Elara Varrin is a dismal failure. Varrin, returning home with a recently hired bodyguard, survives the ambush. The cult's 'Reaper' is wounded in the skirmish and forced to retreat without the prize. The cell has failed to claim its tithe and has now alerted a prominent artisan to their existence.

Xylia's conspiracists examine the collapsed section of the Basilisk Gate wall and declare that the 'unnatural' way the stones fell is proof of a psionic resonance weapon being tested by the 'Shapeshifter' cabal. They distribute pamphlets with their 'findings.'

Lord Perrine Hhune's public condemnation of the Waterdhavian Kontor ends with a whimper. The small group of protesters is quickly and efficiently dispersed by the Kontor's Griffin Guard. The event is so minor it goes completely unnoticed by the general public, a humiliating failure for the society.

Glim-Glim and his followers begin handing out small vials of 'purified' rainwater in the Outer City, warning citizens that the city's water is a 'gnomish mind-control agent.' Most people think they're insane.

The planned defacement of the Amnian-funded mural in Heapside is thwarted. Alerted by a paid informant, a patrol of Amnian Coin-Swords is waiting in the shadows. Calliope's followers are ambushed and chased off before they can throw a single bucket of paint, a public failure for the nativist cause.

Lord Durinbold's 'Heritage Preservation Act' is soundly defeated in the Parliament of Peers. The united voting bloc of the Peerage of Coin, backed by the immense wealth of the Kontors and Consortiums, easily quashes the bill. The defeat is a major blow to the traditionalist faction, demonstrating the waning influence of the old patriar families.

Quest: Organize a Protest at Wyrm's Crossing
Description: Enraged by the beating of an Outer City merchant and the dismissal of their petition, High Watcher Alena organizes a massive, peaceful protest at the southern end of Wyrm's Crossing, demanding an end to the Flaming Fist's corruption. They are joined by the Followers of the Phoenix.

The Free Traders' blockade of the Basilisk Gate is violently broken by the Flaming Fist. After a brief standoff, a large contingent of Fists led by Blaze Liara Portyr storms the blockade, arresting several caravan masters and impounding their wagons. The decisive action reopens the gate but further enrages the Outer City communities.

The druids' night raid on the logging camp fails. The Sembian mercenaries, anticipating a reprisal, had set magical traps and alarms. Elder Elion's circle is ambushed as they approach the camp and are forced to retreat after a brief, sharp skirmish, unable to get close enough to the felled trees to work their magic.

The Ember' leads their growing flock of followers to the protest at Wyrm's Crossing, joining the Hellriders. Their fiery rhetoric and passionate speeches turn a simple protest into a fervent rally for the rights of all the city's outcasts.

The Githyanki investigation into the psychic echoes near their camp is fruitless. The Hand of the Absolute's methods are subtle, leaving no physical or psionic trace for the squad to follow. After a thorough but unsuccessful search of the tenements, the squad returns to K'laar, reporting that the source could not be located and may have been a random, passing phenomenon.

Quest: Publish a Story Blaming Refugees for Merchant's Murder
Description: Ettvard Needle takes Lady Vhage's gold but is unable to run the planned story. With the 'incriminating letter' never planted on the merchant's body by Vhage's bungling cultists, Needle has no 'evidence' to cite. He shelves the story, pocketing the gold and waiting for a more opportune moment, much to Lady Vhage's fury.
